text: Code reviews are an essential practice in software development. They involve team members checking each other's code before it is merged into the main project. One key benefit is catching bugs early. When fresh eyes examine the code, small mistakes and logical errors are often spotted quickly, saving time and effort later. Additionally, code reviews promote knowledge sharing within the team. Developers can learn new techniques, understand different parts of the codebase, and offer constructive feedback. This process ultimately improves team quality. By working together to review code, everyone becomes more accountable and produces better, more reliable software.
